title: US Patent 4718024 - Graphics Data Processing Apparatus (Selectable Pitch)
notes: Asal/Guttag/Novak / TI. Filed 1985-11-05. **Foundational TMS340 architecture patent** - Fig. 2 shows the canonical TMS34010 block diagram (CPU + register files + instruction cache + memory interface + special graphics hardware + host interface + I/O regs + video display controller). Claims raster operations on two image arrays with independently selectable pitches. Cross-references 13 sibling applications filed at the same time covering the rest of the TMS340 architecture (color expand, transparent ops, X/Y coords, draw-and-advance, instruction set, variable field size, etc.).

title: US Patent 5333261 - Graphics Processing Apparatus (X/Y Pixel Location Instruction)
notes: Guttag/Asal/Tebbutt/Novak / TI. Filed 1993-05-07 as a continuation in the original 1985 TMS340 family. Covers an instruction that operates separately on X and Y coordinates of pixel-location registers (the foundation of the '34010 XY-addressing mode).

title: US Patent 5437011 - Graphics Computer System (TMS340 family)
notes: Guttag/Asal/Van Aken/Tebbutt/Novak / TI. Filed 1994-02-04. Continuation in the original 1985 TMS340 family covering the graphics-processor system arrangement (graphics computer system, display system, processor, and method of processing graphic data).

title: US Patent 4747081 - Video Display System with Parallel/Serial Memory (VRAM)
notes: Heilveil/Van Aken/Guttag/Redwine/Pinkham/Novak / TI. Filed 1983-12-30. **Foundational VRAM patent** - the dual-port (parallel + serial) memory architecture that the TMS340 family was designed to drive. The serial-port shift register selected by column address is the canonical Video DRAM mechanism.

title: US Patent 4663735 - Random/Serial Access Mode Selection for Video Memory (VRAM)
notes: Novak/Guttag / TI. Filed 1983-12-30. Companion to US4747081; covers the random/serial access mode selection circuitry for the same Video DRAM.

title: IEEE Chasing Pixels: TI TMS34010 and VRAM (HTML mirror)

title: Avionics and the TMS34020 - Eric Theunissen (tms34020.uav.nl)
